---
title: "EDA"
author: "Peter Xiong"
date: "2025-03-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggOceanMaps)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(ggplot2)
library(gganimate)
```

# Data merging

```{r}
# Define the path to your data directory
data_directory <- "D:/Desktop/PSTAT 197B/scripps/capstone-scripps/data/Specificdate/Temperature"

# List all CSV files
file_paths <- list.files(path = data_directory, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Randomly choose 100 file names from this list for testing
set.seed(123)
selected_files <- sample(file_paths, size = 100)

# Function to read and process each file
process_file <- function(file_path) {
  data <- read_csv(file_path)
  
  # Extract date and depth from the filename
  date_part <- sub(".*_date_(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})\\.csv", "\\1", basename(file_path))
  depth_part <- str_extract(basename(file_path), "-\\d+\\.")
  depth <- as.numeric(str_remove(depth_part, "\\D"))
  date <- as.Date(date_part, format = "%Y-%m-%d")
  
  # Reshape data assuming the first column is 'Row' which contains longitude, other columns are latitudes
  data_long <- pivot_longer(data, 
                            cols = -Row, 
                            names_to = "Latitude", 
                            values_to = "Temperature")
  
  # Convert the 'Row' column to just a longitude identifier
  data_long$Longitude <- str_remove(data_long$Row, "Lon_")
  data_long$Latitude <- str_remove(data_long$Latitude, "Lat_")
  data_long$Date <- date
  data_long$Depth <- depth
  
  # Ensure columns are correctly typed
  data_long$Longitude <- as.numeric(data_long$Longitude)
  data_long$Latitude <- as.numeric(data_long$Latitude)
  
  # Adjust longitude from 0-360 to -180 to 180 if necessary
  data_long$Longitude <- ifelse(data_long$Longitude > 180, data_long$Longitude - 360, data_long$Longitude)
  
  return(data_long)
}

# Process all files and combine into a single dataframe
all_data <- bind_rows(lapply(selected_files, process_file))

# Inspect
head(all_data)
```

# Plot the oceanmaps

```{r}
# Calculate bounding box from your data
lon_range <- range(all_data$Longitude, na.rm = TRUE)
lat_range <- range(all_data$Latitude, na.rm = TRUE)

# basemap() expects a vector in the order c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
my_limits <- c(lon_range[1], lon_range[2], lat_range[1], lat_range[2])

coastline_sf <- ne_coastline(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

static_plot <- basemap(
  limits = my_limits,  
  shapefiles = list(land = coastline_sf),
  bathymetry = FALSE
) +
  geom_point(
    data = all_data,
    aes(
      x = Longitude,
      y = Latitude,
      color = Temperature
    ),
    size = 2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Depth) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  labs(
    title = "Ocean Temperature Observations",
    color = "Temp (°C)",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )

# Print static map
static_plot
```


# Animate maps

```{r}
animated_plot <- static_plot +
  transition_time(all_data$Date) +
  labs(
    title = "Ocean Temperature: {frame_time}"
  )

# Render the animation in the RStudio Viewer
#animated_map <- animate(
#  animated_plot,
#  nframes = 50,  # total frames (adjust as desired)
#  fps = 5,       # frames per second
#  width = 800,
#  height = 500,
#)

#animated_map

# Save as a GIF
# anim_save("ocean_temp_animation.gif", animation = animated_map)
```
